message.reasoning:
We are given: scenario_id: "scenario_00119", opinion_leaf: "Issue_Position_Taxonomy > Information_Integrity_And_Platforms > Trust_In_Mainstream_Journalism". Profile: sex Other, age 59, high neuroticism (77.8% mean), low extraversion (16.7% mean), low conscientiousness (34.1%), moderate agreeableness (63.3%), moderate openness (41.4%). Some notable facets: very high anger/hostility (86%), very high self-consciousness (86%), very low activity level (11%), very low excitement seeking (14%), very low positive emotions (17%). High trust in agreeableness (79%) but also high anxiety and stress vulnerability.

This is trust in mainstream journalism. Given high neuroticism and low extraversion, this person likely has a cynical or wary view of institutions. Low openness (especially ideas 38%) suggests less openness to new perspectives, but trust facet is high (79%). However, anger/hostility is very high, so they might be distrustful of mainstream sources. Also, low conscientiousness (especially dutifulness 23%) suggests less respect for authority or established norms. Age 59 might lean towards older media consumption but also potential skepticism. I lean towards a moderate distrust, not extreme because of high trust facet but balanced by high neuroticism and anger. Let's aim for around -300 to -400. Use non-round numbers. Confidence moderate. Reasoning: profile has high trust but also high neuroticism and anger, leading to mild distrust of mainstream journalism.

Score: -347, confidence: 0.65.
